item.title: Fijiana 7s eye strong start against Japan in Bordeaux tonight
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 05/06/2026 The Fiji Airways Fijiana 7s side will be looking for a strong start against Japan in the final tournament of the World Rugby Sevens Series in France tonight.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 05/06/2026

The Fiji Airways Fijiana 7s side will be looking for a strong start against Japan in the final tournament of the World Rugby Sevens Series in France tonight.

Speaking from France, coach Richard Walker says they played Japan in Hong Kong, but failed to stick to their game plan, which resulted in a loss.

He says they cannot afford to be overconfident against the Japanese side, as they are a very disciplined team.

Walker says success will come down to discipline and ensuring the players support one another on the field.

The coach says one of the biggest lessons the team has learned throughout the season is the importance of consistency.

Insert: Richard Walker on consistency – 5 June 2026

The Fijiana 7s side is currently seventh on the World Rugby Sevens Series standings with 16 points.

Australia leads the standings with 38 points, followed by New Zealand with 36 points, while the USA sits third with 30 points.

The Fijiana will open their Bordeaux 7s campaign against Japan at 10.04 tonight. They will then face Australia at 3.51am tomorrow before taking on Brazil at 11.32pm in their final pool match.

The women's Cup quarter-finals will begin at 1.30am on Sunday, while the semi-finals are scheduled for 10.30pm on Sunday. The final will be played at 3.04am on Monday.

Meanwhile, the Fiji Men's 7s side will open its campaign against Kenya at 12.44am tomorrow before meeting Great Britain at 6.14am in its second pool match.

They will then face South Africa at 9.06pm in their final pool game.

The men's Cup quarter-finals will begin at 3.28am on Sunday, the semi-finals are scheduled for 11.14pm on Sunday, and the final will kick off at 3.41am on Monday.

item.title: Fiji Rugby strengthens commitment to player welfare with new foundation
item.description: Today is more than the launch of new initiatives; it marks a significant milestone in the continuing evolution of Fiji Rugby and Fiji Rugby's commitment to building a stronger, more sustainable future for our players, our communities, and our nation. This was shared by FRU Chairman, John Sanday, during the launch of the Life After Rugby Support in Suva.
item.content:encoded:
Today is more than the launch of new initiatives; it marks a significant milestone in the continuing evolution of Fiji Rugby and Fiji Rugby's commitment to building a stronger, more sustainable future for our players, our communities, and our nation.

This was shared by FRU Chairman, John Sanday, during the launch of the Life After Rugby Support in Suva.

Sanday, a former Flying Fijian loose forward, says for generations, rugby has been woven into the fabric of Fiji, and it has brought pride and recognition to our country, united our people across villages and communities, inspired young Fijians to dream big, and provided opportunities for many of our finest athletes to represent Fiji on the world stage.

He says the true strength of any rugby organization is measured not only by its achievements in competition, but by how it cares for its people throughout their lives.

The Chairman says they are also proudly launching the Fiji Rakavi Foundation — a commitment to ensuring that our players, both men and women, continue to receive support long after their playing careers have ended.

He says through the Foundation, they aim to provide access to education pathways, medical assistance, rehabilitation services, transition programmes, and welfare support for former players who have given so much in service to Fiji Rugby.

Sanday says they also celebrate the formal partnership between the Fiji Rugby Union and the Australia Pacific Management and Training, and t his partnership will create meaningful education and training opportunities that equip our players and rugby communities with skills for life beyond rugby t hrough structured learning programmes, professional development pathways, and recognized certifications.

He says in addition, they launch the Fiji Rugby Management System , a modern platform that will strengthen governance, improve player registration and welfare management, enhance programme delivery, and support informed d ecision-making through reliable data and accountability.

Sanday says while these initiatives are diverse in purpose, they are united by one common principle putting people first .

item.title: Naholo set for Hurricanes against Brumbies as qualifying finals kicks off tonight
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 05/06/2026 Kini Naholo will line up for the Hurricanes when they take on the Brumbies tonight in the first 2026 Shop N Save Super Rugby Pacific qualifying final.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 05/06/2026

Kini Naholo will line up for the Hurricanes when they take on the Brumbies tonight in the first 2026 Shop N Save Super Rugby Pacific qualifying final.

After finishing the regular season in first place, the Hurricanes will be hoping for a win to secure a spot in the next round.

Asafo Aumua, Pasilio Tosi and Xavier Numia will start in the front row, with Caleb Delany teaming up with Warner Dearns in the second row.

Co-captain Du'Plessis Kirifi returns from a shoulder injury to start at openside flanker and forms a loose forward trio with Brad Shields at blindside flanker and Peter Lakai at No. 8 .

Cam Roigard resumes his halves partnership with first five-eighth Ruben Love, while co-captain Jordie Barrett has overcome a hamstring injury to start at second five-eighth alongside centre Billy Proctor.

Meanwhile, the Brumbies will travel to Wellington with a point to prove.

The Australian side knows what it takes to bring down New Zealand opposition, having already recorded victories over the Crusaders, Chiefs, Blues and Highlanders this season.

Rob Valetini will partner with Luke Reimer and Charlie Cale in the loose forwards.

Declan Meredith will once again team up with Ryan Lonergan in the halves , with the duo looking to create space and unleash their outside backs.

The Brumbies will take on the Hurricanes at 7.05pm tonight.

In other qualifying finals, the Crusaders will host the Blues at 4.35pm tomorrow, while the Chiefs will meet the Reds at 7.05pm.

HURRICANES (1-15): Xavier Numia, Asafo Aumua, Pasilio Tosi, Caleb Delany, Warner Dearns, Brad Shields, Du'Plessis Kirifi (co-c), Peter Lakai, Cam Roigard, Ruben Love, Kini Naholo, Jordie Barrett (co-c), Billy Proctor, Josh Moorby, Callum Harkin

Replacements: Raymond Tuputupu, Pouri Rakete-Stones, Tyrel Lomax, Isaia Walker-Leawere, Brayden Iose, Ereatara Enari, Jone Rova, Ngane Punivai

BRUMBIES (1-15): James Slipper, Lachlan Lonergan, Allan Alaalatoa (c), Lachlan Shaw, Cadeyrn Neville, Rob Valetini, Luke Reimer, Charlie Cale, Ryan Lonergan, Declan Meredith, Corey Toole, David Feliuai, Hudson Creighton, Andy Muirhead, Tom Wright

Replacements: Billy Pollard, Blake Schoupp, Rhys van Nek, Nick Frost, Rory Scott, Klayton Thorn, Tane Edmed, Ollie Sapsford

item.title: There is no place for drugs in schools and in the Deans - Radrodro
item.description: There is no place for drugs in schools or in the Vodafone Deans Trophy competition this season. That was the clear message from Fiji's Minister for Education, Aseri Radrodro, during the launch of the Deans Trophy competition at Gospel High School.
item.content:encoded:
There is no place for drugs in schools or in the Vodafone Deans Trophy competition this season.

That was the clear message from Fiji's Minister for Education, Aseri Radrodro, during the launch of the Deans Trophy competition at Gospel High School.

Radrodro says more than 121 schools will participate this year, reflecting the continued growth of the prestigious schools rugby tournament.

He says that as participation increases, so too does the responsibility of everyone involved to uphold the values of the competition and ensure a safe environment for all participants.

The Minister stressed that every student deserves to compete in a setting that promotes safety, respect, and fair play.

He urged students to wear their school jerseys with pride and to say no to drugs both on and off the field.

Radrodro also reminded students that they represent the future of Fiji rugby, adding that the Deans competition serves as an important pathway for developing the country's next generation of rugby players.

item.title: Friends to foes: Anton Segner, Leicester Fainga'anuku set for Super Rugby showdown
item.description: When Anton Segner arrived in New Zealand, Leicester Fainga'anuku was his first friend.Now, nearly a decade later, he is his latest

item.title: Lautoka and Nasinu to kick off 2026 BiC Fiji FACT
item.description: The stage is set for an exciting start to the 2026 BiC Fiji FACT as Lautoka FC and Nasinu FC prepare to clash in the opening match of the tournament at Subrail Park in Labasa on 19th June. With pride, bragging rights and the first major trophy of the season on the line , eight of Fiji's top district teams will battle it out over two weekends in what is expected to be a thrilling competition.
item.content:encoded:
The stage is set for an exciting start to the 2026 BiC Fiji FACT as Lautoka FC and Nasinu FC prepare to clash in the opening match of the tournament at Subrail Park in Labasa on 19th June.

With pride, bragging rights and the first major trophy of the season on the line , eight of Fiji's top district teams will battle it out over two weekends in what is expected to be a thrilling competition.

Fiji Football Association CEO Mohammed Yusuf says anticipation is growing as teams arrive with strengthened squads and high expectations.

Yusuf says fans can expect quality football, exciting new signings and fierce competition throughout the tournament.

The excitement is already being felt off the field, with VVIP tickets nearly sold out as supporters rush to secure their seats for one of Fiji football's biggest events.

Group A features defending champions Labasa, Rewa, Suva and Navua, while Lautoka, Ba, Nasinu and Nadroga make up Group B.

Lautoka will face Nasinu at 1pm in the opener, while traditional giants Ba will play Nadroga at 3pm, Rewa will face Navua at 5pm, while hosts Labasa will take on Suva under lights at 7.30pm.

The pool matches will be played from 19th to 21st June before the action returns for the semi-finals on 27th June and the grand final on 28th June at Subrail Park.

item.title: Bula Boys depart for Vanuatu ahead of FIFA friendlies
item.description: The Fiji national side has departed for Port Vila with a 20-member squad featuring a mix of experienced players and emerging talent as preparations continue for upcoming international competitions. Auvray says the matches will provide an important opportunity to assess players in an international environme nt and identify those capable of leading Fiji football into the future.
item.content:encoded:
The Fiji national side has departed for Port Vila with a 20-member squad featuring a mix of experienced players and emerging talent as preparations continue for upcoming international competitions.

Auvray says the matches will provide an important opportunity to assess players in an international environme nt and identify those capable of leading Fiji football into the future.

Veteran striker Roy Krishna headlines the squad.

The squad includes Isikeli Sevanaia, Melvin Prakash, Delon Shankar, Ivan Kumar, Lekima Gonerau, Gabiriele Matainisiga, Semi Nabenu, Tevita Waranaivalu, Thomas Dunn, Ryan Naresh, Ilisoni Logaivau, Rusiate Doidoi, Maikah Dau, Josaia Sela, Zacariah Harang, Ibraheem Afazal, Solayman Mohammed, Roy Krishna, Setareki Hughes and Asivorosi Rabo.

The matches will be played this Saturday and next Tuesday.

item.title: Barrett ruled out as Blues name squad for Crusaders clash
item.description: The Blues will head into this week's 2026 Shop N Save Super Rugby Pacific qualifying final against the Crusaders without Beauden B
